IsA: It is one of Shakespeare's greatest works.
It is one of Shakespeare's most popular plays.
HasA: It has two of Shakespeare's four longest roles.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its unusual mechanics.
It is characterized by its treatment of race.
It is characterized by the motivations of Iago and his relationship to Othello.
UsedFor: It is used for theatrical performances.
It is used for stage adaptations.
It is used for screen adaptations.
It is used for operatic adaptations.
OriginatedIn: It originated in a story by Cinthio.
It originated in Gli Hecatommithi by Cinthio.
CreatedBy: It was created by William Shakespeare.
PublishedIn: It was published in one quarto edition from 1622.
It was published in the First Folio.
WrittenBy: It was written by William Shakespeare.
PerformedBy: It was performed by white actors in dark makeup.
It was performed by black actors in the 19th century.
PartOf: It is part of Shakespeare's major tragedies.
KnownFor: It is known for its demanding and desirable roles.
It is known for the manipulation of Othello by Iago.
It is known for the theme of infidelity.
It is known for its critical attention on the nature of the play's tragedy.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Macbeth.
It is associated with King Lear.
It is associated with Hamlet.
It is associated with William Shakespeare.
It is associated with Cinthio.
It is associated with Iago.
It is associated with Desdemona.
It is associated with Roderigo.
It is associated with Cassio.
It is associated with Emilia.
About: It is about the Moorish military commander Othello.
It is about Othello being manipulated by his ensign Iago.
It is about Othello suspecting his wife Desdemona of infidelity.
It is about the tragic downfall of Othello.
It is about Iago's revenge plot.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Virgil's Aeneid.
It was influenced by Ovid's Metamorphoses.
It was influenced by Chaucer's The Canterbury Tales.
It was influenced by Geoffrey Fenton's Certaine Tragicall Discourses.
It was influenced by Kyd's The Spanish Tragedy.
It was influenced by George Peele's The Battle of Alcazar.
It was influenced by the anonymous Arden of Faversham.
It was influenced by Marlowe's Doctor Faustus.
It was influenced by Heywood's A Woman Killed with Kindness.
It was influenced by Shakespeare's own earlier plays.
It was influenced by the Moorish ambassador of the King of Barbary.
It was influenced by Gasparo Contarini's Commonwealth and Government of Venice.
It was influenced by Leo Africanus's A Geographical Historie of Africa.
